Summary: Project scaffolding: Initialize Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind
Description: Set up Next.js 14 App Router project with TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4, ESLint, Prettier. Configure path aliases, env handling (.env.local), and folder structure (app/, lib/, components/, types/).

Summary: Database schema: ContentFlow data model with Drizzle ORM
Description: Design and implement PostgreSQL schema via Drizzle ORM: users, workspaces, tables, records, fields, views, activity_log. Include migrations setup. Use Supabase Postgres for managed DB.

Summary: Authentication: Clerk integration for user management
Description: Integrate Clerk for sign-up/sign-in, session management, and user profiles. Configure webhooks for user lifecycle events. Support Google, GitHub, email magic link providers.

Summary: Real-time data layer: Supabase Realtime for live collaboration
Description: Set up Supabase Realtime subscriptions for record changes, field updates, and view state sync. Implement optimistic updates with conflict resolution.

Summary: App shell: Layout with sidebar navigation and workspace switcher
Description: Build responsive app shell: collapsible sidebar (workspace list, table list, view list), top bar (breadcrumbs, search, user menu), main content area. Match Airtable layout patterns from current index.html.

Summary: Design system: Build token-based design system from current CSS
Description: Extract current CSS custom properties into a proper design system: color tokens, typography scale, spacing, radii, shadows. Create reusable component primitives (Button, Input, Select, Badge, Avatar). Support light/dark themes.

Summary: API layer: REST/Trpc endpoints for CRUD operations
Description: Implement API routes for workspace, table, record, and field CRUD. Use tRPC for type-safe API contracts. Include input validation, error handling, and rate limiting.

Summary: Data seeding: Editorial sample data and migration scripts
Description: Create seed scripts that populate a new workspace with the 18-sample editorial records from current index.html. Include realistic content types, statuses, assignees, and dates.

Summary: Grid view: Editable spreadsheet with column types and inline edit
Description: Port and enhance the Grid view from index.html into a React component. Implement: sticky headers, column resize, inline cell editing (text, select, date, number, checkbox, collaborator), row selection, column reorder. Use virtual scrolling for large datasets.

Summary: Kanban view: Drag-and-drop card board by status column
Description: Port Kanban view with real drag-and-drop (use @dnd-kit). Cards grouped by status with: title, type tag, assignee avatar, due date, word count. Support adding new cards per column. Smooth animations.

Summary: Calendar view: Monthly publish schedule with event placement
Description: Port Calendar view: monthly grid, events placed on publish dates, color-coded by status or type. Navigation (prev/next month, today button). Event click opens record detail. Support week view toggle.

Summary: Record detail panel: Slide-in editor with all field types
Description: Slide-in panel (from right) showing all record fields with appropriate editors. Status/type dropdowns, date picker, collaborator picker, rich text notes, word count auto-calc. Keyboard shortcut (Esc to close).

Summary: Search and filter: Global search with field-level filtering
Description: Implement debounced search across all text fields. Filter bar with: status filter (multi-select), type filter, assignee filter, date range filter, featured toggle. Combine filters with AND logic. Persist filter state in URL.

Summary: Sort and group: Column sorting and record grouping
Description: Click column headers to sort (asc/desc/none). Group records by any field (status, type, assignee, date). Group headers show count and collapse/expand. Sort within groups.

Summary: Workspace and table management: Create/switch/delete workspaces
Description: Multi-workspace support: create workspace, add tables to workspace, rename/delete tables, invite members. Workspace settings (name, icon, description). Table CRUD with field definition.

Summary: Field type system: Configurable field definitions per table
Description: Field type registry: text, long text, number, select, multi-select, date, date range, checkbox, collaborator, URL, email, phone, attachment, formula. Add/edit/delete/reorder fields. Field validation rules.

Summary: AI content suggestions: Title
Workflow Status: Integrate OpenAI API for: auto-generate blog post titles from topic, suggest content outlines, recommend tags/categories, generate social media snippets from long-form content. Use streaming for responsive UX.

Summary: Content templates: Pre-built editorial workflow templates
Description: Template system: pre-built templates (Blog Post Pipeline, Newsletter Workflow, Social Campaign, Video Production, Podcast Series). Each template defines fields, statuses, sample records. Users can create/save custom templates.

Summary: Activity log and audit trail: Track all record changes
Description: Log every field change with timestamp, user, old value, new value. Display in record detail panel as timeline. Workspace-level activity feed. Support for undo (revert last change).

Summary: Keyboard shortcuts: Power-user keyboard navigation
Description: Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+K command palette, Cmd+N new record, Cmd+F search, arrow keys navigate grid, Enter opens record, Escape closes panel, Cmd+Z undo. Shortcut help overlay (Cmd+/).

Summary: Import/Export: CSV and JSON data import/export
Description: Import data from CSV (auto-detect columns), JSON, or paste from spreadsheet. Export table as CSV, JSON, or shareable link. Handle large files (10K+ rows) with chunked processing.

Summary: Attachments: File upload and image management
Description: File upload to S3/R2 bucket. Image thumbnails in grid and kanban. Drag-and-drop upload. File preview modal. Storage limits per plan tier. Support for images, PDFs, videos, documents.

Summary: Views management: Save/switch/delete custom views
Description: Save current filter/sort/group/view-type as named views. Switch between views instantly. Share views with team. Default view per table. View permissions (private/shared).

Summary: Email notifications: Record changes and mentions
Description: Email notifications for: @mention in notes, record assigned to you, status change on followed records, weekly digest of workspace activity. Configurable per-user notification preferences.

Summary: Integrations: Zapier/Make webhook and API connector
Description: REST API with API keys for external integrations. Webhook support for record create/update/delete events. Zapier/Make integration for workflow automation. Slack notifications integration.

Summary: Content calendar publish: Social media scheduling integration
Description: Connect to social platforms (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram) for direct scheduling from ContentFlow. Auto-post on publish date. Cross-platform content repurposing (blog → social snippets). Content calendar sync with Google Calendar.

Summary: Analytics dashboard: Content performance metrics
Description: Dashboard showing: content production velocity, status distribution, team workload, publish date compliance, word count trends. Per-record analytics (if integrated with CMS: views, engagement).

Summary: Comments and collaboration: Inline commenting on records
Description: Threaded comments on records. @mention teammates. Comment resolution (mark as done). Comment notifications. Rich text in comments (bold, links, code). Comment history.

Summary: Formulas and computed fields: Auto-calculate derived values
Description: Formula field type: calculate word count from notes, days until publish, status progress percentage. Support basic functions (SUM, COUNT, IF, CONCAT, DATE_DIFF). Cell-level formulas like spreadsheets.

Summary: Record linking: Relational fields between tables
Description: Link records across tables (e.g., Blog Post → Author → Campaign). Lookup fields that pull related record data. Rollup fields (count, sum, list of linked records). Junction tables for many-to-many.

Summary: AI content assistant: In-context writing help
Description: AI assistant that can: rewrite selected text (tone: formal/casual/concise), expand bullet points into paragraphs, summarize long notes, suggest SEO keywords, generate meta descriptions. Stream responses inline.

Summary: Workflow automations: Trigger actions on conditions
Description: If-this-then-that style automations: when status changes to 'Published', notify team + update analytics. When date arrives, send reminder. Auto-assign based on content type. Custom automation builder UI.

Summary: Content repurposing tracker: Multi-channel content mapping
Description: Track how content flows across channels: Blog Post → Social Posts → Newsletter → Podcast. Visual content tree showing parent/child content. Status sync (if original updated, flag derivatives). ROI per content piece.

Summary: Mobile responsive: Optimized mobile and tablet experience
Description: Mobile-optimized layout: stacked views, touch-friendly controls, swipe gestures on kanban cards, bottom sheet for record detail. Progressive enhancement for tablet. PWA support for offline access.

Summary: Performance optimization: Handle 100K+ records smoothly
Description: Virtual scrolling for grid (react-window). Lazy loading for kanban columns. Optimistic updates with rollback. Database query optimization (indexes, pagination). CDN for static assets. Bundle size < 200KB gzipped.

Summary: Testing suite: Unit
Workflow Status: Vitest for unit tests (components, utilities, API). Playwright for E2E tests (view switching, record CRUD, drag-and-drop, search/filter). Test fixtures with sample data. CI integration with GitHub Actions.

Summary: Documentation: User docs
Workflow Status: User documentation site (Next.js or Docusaurus). API reference docs. Interactive onboarding flow for new users. Video tutorials for key features. Changelog and release notes.

Summary: Accessibility audit: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
Description: Full accessibility audit: screen reader support, keyboard navigation for all features, color contrast (4.5:1 minimum), focus management, ARIA labels, reduced motion support. Automated axe-core testing in CI.

Summary: Deployment pipeline: Vercel + CI/CD setup
Description: Vercel deployment with preview branches. GitHub Actions CI: lint, typecheck, test, build. Environment variable management. Database migration automation. Staging + production environments.

Summary: Stripe billing: Subscription management and payments
Description: Stripe integration: subscription tiers (Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise), usage-based billing for API calls, team seat management, invoice generation, trial periods, upgrade/downgrade flows. Webhook handling for payment events.

Summary: Landing page and marketing site
Description: Marketing landing page: hero, features showcase, pricing table, testimonials, FAQ, CTA. Blog for SEO content. Product screenshots and demo video. Social proof and trust signals.

Summary: Launch preparation: Beta testing and bug fixes
Description: Closed beta with 50-100 users. Bug bash session. Performance profiling. Security audit (OWASP top 10). Load testing (k6 or Artillery). Monitoring setup (Sentry, analytics). Launch checklist.

Summary: Public launch: Product Hunt + community launch
Description: Product Hunt launch preparation. Hacker News Show HN post. Social media launch campaign. Beta user testimonials. Launch week content calendar. Press kit.

Summary: Post-launch iteration: User feedback and quick wins
Description: Collect user feedback (in-app widget, support emails). Triage bug reports. Implement quick-win features from beta feedback. Iterate on onboarding flow based on drop-off data. Weekly release cadence.
